title: Apple Defeats Liability for Not Scanning iCloud for CSAM, But the Judge Was Not Pleased–Amy v. Apple
excerpt: This case involves Apple’s handling of user-uploaded files hosted in private iCloud storage. Instead of adopting PhotoDNA to scan hosted files for CSAM, Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good. So Apple U-turned on its...
content_html:
This case involves Apple’s handling of user-uploaded files hosted in private iCloud storage. Instead of adopting PhotoDNA to scan hosted files for CSAM, Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good. So Apple U-turned on its efforts to scan for CSAM in its cloud storage. Instead, Apple implemented end-to-end encryption for iCloud files.

Apple’s manuevers confused the public and seemed like an embarrassing unforced error for Apple. It also ensured pressure from governments and plaintiffs, including CSAM victims, who preferred Apple’s more interventionist approaches, which Apple had voluntarily demonstrated it was willing to do.

This lawsuit represents a full-scale attack on Apple and Section 230. “Plaintiffs allege that Apple’s failure to implement any known CSAM detection is a design defect because Apple can safely implement readily available features to prevent the spread of known CSAM but has continuously failed to do so.” Prior blog post. The court dismisses the third amended complaint, which tees this case up for the Ninth Circuit, where (as usual) anything could happen.

* * *

The court reiterates that Section 230 applies to the plaintiffs’ claims:

First, Plaintiffs’ claims treat Apple as a publisher or speaker of the CSAM content that animates Plaintiffs’ injuries. Fundamentally, Plaintiffs contend that Apple has elected to permit users to disseminate and share third-party CSAM content when it could have—and, in their view, should have—used readily available technology to prevent the distribution of child pornography depicting the Plaintiffs in this putative class. The duties Plaintiffs seek to invoke “spring[ ] from the defendant’s status as publisher,” and consequently, “immunity applies.” Second, immunity also applies because “the means to avoid liability requires [Apple] to act as a publisher.” As a result, Apple is entitled to complete immunity under § 230.

Citing Doe 1 v. Meta, the court says:

Plaintiffs’ injuries are the direct result of the actions of third parties who used iCloud to share CSAM, a use Apple neither explicitly condones nor prevents (even assuming—as alleged in the TAC—that Apple was aware of the use of iCloud for this purpose)….though Plaintiffs allege that Apple knew that its tools were likely to be used to distribute child pornography (as confirmed by the internal Apple text messages at the center of this case), under the current state of the law, Apple is still entitled to immunity under § 230—irrespective of that general knowledge….

Plaintiffs cannot avoid the fact that a tool that detects CSAM must review CSAM to make such a determination. And while Apple could have taken steps to do so—as its competitors have done by using PhotoDNA—Grindr confirms that § 230 bars claims arising from the design decisions Apple could have taken where those claims relate to Apple’s role facilitating the communication and content of others

(A reminder that the defendant’s scienter is irrelevant to Section 230).

The plaintiffs tried to fit into the new Section 230 exceptions created in Doe v. Twitter, but the court rebuffs the move:

This case does not concern or even discuss Apple’s content reporting systems; it concerns Apple’s “failure to implement industry-standard safeguards” against the dissemination of CSAM. Though reporting systems and CSAM safeguards may both be described as “defects,” the latter requires the Court to treat Apple as a publisher. Twitter “could fulfill its purported duty to cure reporting infrastructure deficiencies without monitoring, removing, or in any way engaging with third-party content”; Apple cannot fulfill a duty to institute CSAM safeguards without deploying a tool like NeuralHash or PhotoDNA. Both NeuralHash and PhotoDNA were built to monitor and report violative images uploaded to company servers. Yet just the decision regarding whether to deploy either tool is a choice related to content moderation.

Also, Apple didn’t fail to satisfy any duty to report items to NCMEC if it never identified CSAM in the first place.

The Lemmon v. Snap workaround fails: “all of Plaintiffs’ claims here are inexorably linked to third-party content; Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple created content like a Snapchat filter that caused them harm.” The Roommates.com workaround also fails: “Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple modified or augmented the CSAM on its servers in any way.”

* * *

The case reaches its inevitable denouement of a win for Apple. However, Judge Wise remains troubled about its implications. She expresses her uneasiness in stronger-than-normal terms:

As it stands, nothing in the law prevents any company, including Apple, from utilizing available technology or creating new technology to identify and report child pornography stored and distributed on their traditional servers or through their cloud services. Conversely, there is no law that obligates companies to proactively do so. Undoubtedly any such legislation would come at a cost of at least some loss of privacy for millions of people. But if lawmakers expected that companies would take steps to prevent their products from being used for storing and distributing child pornography based on something short of a legal imperative, this case, like many others before it, demonstrates the inadequacy of that approach. If lawmakers want to ensure that Apple and other companies address their role in the dissemination of CSAM, they must require it under the law. In other words, lawmakers can fix this problem that is contributing to the exploitation of children.

She goes through this framing pretty quickly, but we should slow it down. The “loss of privacy for millions of people” she briefly references deserves a little more care. The opinion downplays the encryption angle; it mentions encryption only twice, as if it’s an afterthought. However, encryption is the critical attribute underlying Apple’s moves. Forcing Apple to scan for CSAM in iCloud means breaking end-to-end encryption for everyone and all purposes, including: malefactors who would intercept private files for criminal purposes; and government actors who have repeatedly shown that they will break into, distribute, and weaponize privately stored file caches (I was just thinking about the North Korea Sony hack. Remember it?).

So yes, the “privacy loss” Judge Wise mentions indeed would be a major cost to everyone. Worse, it would ensure new victims have their sensitive, private, or abusive images nonconsensually intercepted and disseminated. (Do you recall the Fappening and Snappening?) End-to-end encryption isn’t just some nice-to-have feature; it is one of the core planks of a technology architecture that keeps people safer.

Judge Wise continues:

This Order does not turn on whether Apple’s decisions contributed to Plaintiffs’ injuries. All Plaintiffs’ claims are founded on Apple serving as a publisher of third-party content. It is that role as “publisher” that is dispositive on the issue of immunity. This does not mean that the existence of images and videos of the putative class members being sexually abused—content that they allege is regularly stored and disseminated on iCloud—has not caused Plaintiffs real and lasting harm…

the outcome also adds credence to claims that the Ninth Circuit “has expanded § 230(c)’s scope to provide functional immunity to internet companies, even when they are aware (or should be aware) of unlawful content on their websites.” The practical outcome is that the current state of the law prioritizes privacy—a laudable and critically important value given that in our modern world nearly all our most personal and intimate data (including financial and health records) are stored and transmitted online. But the law should not ignore how those who create, view, and distribute child pornography leverage privacy protections to avoid detection by law enforcement. In the current legal framework, there is no protection for members of the putative class—individuals who as children were photographed and filmed while being abused in the vilest ways imaginable, and who now are repeatedly victimized each time the intimate and tortured images of their trauma are distributed to others. Those children are the collateral damage of our ineffective legal landscape. They deserve better.

Judge Wise isn’t well-situated to compare the relative strengths and limitations of the full range of potential anti-CSAM options, but a leading tool has always been and remains the government’s efforts to find and prosecute the creators, disseminators, and downloaders of CSAM. (Do you recall the federal government’s choices that leave children more vulnerable?) Making sure the government is doing what it can should be the #1 priority. In contrast, breaking end-to-end encryption shouldn’t be anywhere in the regulatory toolkit.

Case Citation: Amy v. Apple Inc., 2026 WL 2031817 (N.D. Cal. July 13, 2026). The complaint.

blocks.html_en: This case involves Apple’s handling of user-uploaded files hosted in private iCloud storage. Instead of adopting PhotoDNA to scan hosted files for CSAM, Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good. So Apple U-turned on its efforts to scan for CSAM in its cloud storage. Instead, Apple implemented end-to-end encryption for iCloud files.

sentences.en: This case involves Apple’s handling of user-uploaded files hosted in private iCloud storage.
sentences.zh: 此案涉及苹果对私有 iCloud 存储中托管的用户上传文件的处理。

sentences.en: Instead of adopting PhotoDNA to scan hosted files for CSAM, Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good.
sentences.zh: Apple 没有采用 PhotoDNA 扫描托管文件中的 CSAM，而是创建了自己的专有替代方案 NeuralHash，但显然效果不太好。

sentences.en: So Apple U-turned on its efforts to scan for CSAM in its cloud storage.
sentences.zh: 因此，Apple 开始在其云存储中扫描 CSAM。

sentences.en: Instead, Apple implemented end-to-end encryption for iCloud files.
sentences.zh: 相反，Apple 对 iCloud 文件实施了端到端加密。

blocks.html_en: Apple’s manuevers confused the public and seemed like an embarrassing unforced error for Apple. It also ensured pressure from governments and plaintiffs, including CSAM victims, who preferred Apple’s more interventionist approaches, which Apple had voluntarily demonstrated it was willing to do.

sentences.en: Apple’s manuevers confused the public and seemed like an embarrassing unforced error for Apple.
sentences.zh: 苹果公司的举动让公众感到困惑，对于苹果公司来说，这似乎是一个令人尴尬的非受迫性错误。

sentences.en: It also ensured pressure from governments and plaintiffs, including CSAM victims, who preferred Apple’s more interventionist approaches, which Apple had voluntarily demonstrated it was willing to do.
sentences.zh: 它还确保了来自政府和原告（包括 CSAM 受害者）的压力，他们更喜欢苹果更具干预性的做法，而苹果已经自愿表明愿意这样做。

blocks.html_en: This lawsuit represents a full-scale attack on Apple and Section 230. “Plaintiffs allege that Apple’s failure to implement any known CSAM detection is a design defect because Apple can safely implement readily available features to prevent the spread of known CSAM but has continuously failed to do so.” Prior blog post. The court dismisses the third amended complaint, which tees this case up for the Ninth Circuit, where (as usual) anything could happen.

sentences.en: This lawsuit represents a full-scale attack on Apple and Section 230.
sentences.zh: 这起诉讼是对苹果和 230 条款的全面攻击。

sentences.en: “Plaintiffs allege that Apple’s failure to implement any known CSAM detection is a design defect because Apple can safely implement readily available features to prevent the spread of known CSAM but has continuously failed to do so.”
sentences.zh: “原告声称，苹果未能实施任何已知的 CSAM 检测是一种设计缺陷，因为苹果可以安全地实施现成的功能来防止已知 CSAM 的传播，但一直未能这样做。”

sentences.en: Prior blog post.
sentences.zh: 之前的博客文章。

sentences.en: The court dismisses the third amended complaint, which tees this case up for the Ninth Circuit, where (as usual) anything could happen.
sentences.zh: 法院驳回了第三次修改后的申诉，该申诉将案件提交第九巡回法院审理，在那里（像往常一样）任何事情都可能发生。

blocks.html_en: The court reiterates that Section 230 applies to the plaintiffs’ claims:

sentences.en: The court reiterates that Section 230 applies to the plaintiffs’ claims:
sentences.zh: 法院重申第 230 条适用于原告的索赔：

blocks.html_en: First, Plaintiffs’ claims treat Apple as a publisher or speaker of the CSAM content that animates Plaintiffs’ injuries. Fundamentally, Plaintiffs contend that Apple has elected to permit users to disseminate and share third-party CSAM content when it could have—and, in their view, should have—used readily available technology to prevent the distribution of child pornography depicting the Plaintiffs in this putative class. The duties Plaintiffs seek to invoke “spring[ ] from the defendant’s status as publisher,” and consequently, “immunity applies.” Second, immunity also applies because “the means to avoid liability requires [Apple] to act as a publisher.” As a result, Apple is entitled to complete immunity under § 230.

sentences.en: First, Plaintiffs’ claims treat Apple as a publisher or speaker of the CSAM content that animates Plaintiffs’ injuries.
sentences.zh: 首先，原告的主张将苹果视为 CSAM 内容的发布者或发言人，这些内容激起了原告的伤害。

sentences.en: Fundamentally, Plaintiffs contend that Apple has elected to permit users to disseminate and share third-party CSAM content when it could have—and, in their view, should have—used readily available technology to prevent the distribution of child pornography depicting the Plaintiffs in this putative class.
sentences.zh: 从根本上说，原告认为，苹果公司本来可以（并且在他们看来应该）使用现成的技术来防止传播描绘此类推定类别中的原告的儿童色情内容，但苹果公司已选择允许用户传播和共享第三方 CSAM 内容。

sentences.en: The duties Plaintiffs seek to invoke “spring[ ] from the defendant’s status as publisher,” and consequently, “immunity applies.”
sentences.zh: 原告寻求援引“被告作为出版商的身份”的义务，因此“适用豁免权”。

sentences.en: Second, immunity also applies because “the means to avoid liability requires [Apple] to act as a publisher.”
sentences.zh: 其次，豁免权也适用，因为“避免责任的方法需要[苹果]充当发行商。”

sentences.en: As a result, Apple is entitled to complete immunity under § 230.
sentences.zh: 因此，Apple 有权根据第 230 条享有完全豁免。

blocks.html_en: The plaintiffs tried to fit into the new Section 230 exceptions created in Doe v. Twitter, but the court rebuffs the move:

sentences.en: The plaintiffs tried to fit into the new Section 230 exceptions created in Doe v.
sentences.zh: 原告试图适应 Doe v. 案中创建的新的第 230 条例外。

sentences.en: Twitter, but the court rebuffs the move:
sentences.zh: Twitter，但法院拒绝了此举：

blocks.html_en: This case does not concern or even discuss Apple’s content reporting systems; it concerns Apple’s “failure to implement industry-standard safeguards” against the dissemination of CSAM. Though reporting systems and CSAM safeguards may both be described as “defects,” the latter requires the Court to treat Apple as a publisher. Twitter “could fulfill its purported duty to cure reporting infrastructure deficiencies without monitoring, removing, or in any way engaging with third-party content”; Apple cannot fulfill a duty to institute CSAM safeguards without deploying a tool like NeuralHash or PhotoDNA. Both NeuralHash and PhotoDNA were built to monitor and report violative images uploaded to company servers. Yet just the decision regarding whether to deploy either tool is a choice related to content moderation.

sentences.en: This case does not concern or even discuss Apple’s content reporting systems; it concerns Apple’s “failure to implement industry-standard safeguards” against the dissemination of CSAM.
sentences.zh: 本案不涉及、甚至不讨论苹果的内容报告系统；它涉及苹果“未能针对 CSAM 传播实施行业标准保障措施”。

sentences.en: Though reporting systems and CSAM safeguards may both be described as “defects,” the latter requires the Court to treat Apple as a publisher.
sentences.zh: 尽管报告系统和 CSAM 保障措施都可以被描述为“缺陷”，但后者要求法院将 Apple 视为发行商。

sentences.en: Twitter “could fulfill its purported duty to cure reporting infrastructure deficiencies without monitoring, removing, or in any way engaging with third-party content”; Apple cannot fulfill a duty to institute CSAM safeguards without deploying a tool like NeuralHash or PhotoDNA.
sentences.zh: Twitter“可以履行其声称的职责，解决报告基础设施的缺陷，而无需监控、删除或以任何方式参与第三方内容”；如果不部署 NeuralHash 或 PhotoDNA 等工具，Apple 就无法履行建立 CSAM 保护措施的责任。

sentences.en: Both NeuralHash and PhotoDNA were built to monitor and report violative images uploaded to company servers.
sentences.zh: NeuralHash 和 PhotoDNA 都是为了监控和报告上传到公司服务器的违规图像而构建的。

sentences.en: Yet just the decision regarding whether to deploy either tool is a choice related to content moderation.
sentences.zh: 然而，是否部署任一工具的决定只是与内容审核相关的选择。

blocks.html_en: This case does not concern or even discuss Apple’s content reporting systems; it concerns Apple’s “failure to implement industry-standard safeguards” against the dissemination of CSAM. Though reporting systems and CSAM safeguards may both be described as “defects,” the latter requires the Court to treat Apple as a publisher. Twitter “could fulfill its purported duty to cure reporting infrastructure deficiencies without monitoring, removing, or in any way engaging with third-party content”; Apple cannot fulfill a duty to institute CSAM safeguards without deploying a tool like NeuralHash or PhotoDNA. Both NeuralHash and PhotoDNA were built to monitor and report violative images uploaded to company servers. Yet just the decision regarding whether to deploy either tool is a choice related to content moderation.

sentences.zh: 尽管报告系统和 CSAM 保障措施都可以被描述为“缺陷”，但后者要求法院将 Apple 视为发行商。

sentences.en: Twitter “could fulfill its purported duty to cure reporting infrastructure deficiencies without monitoring, removing, or in any way engaging with third-party content”; Apple cannot fulfill a duty to institute CSAM safeguards without deploying a tool like NeuralHash or PhotoDNA.
sentences.zh: Twitter“可以履行其声称的职责，解决报告基础设施的缺陷，而无需监控、删除或以任何方式参与第三方内容”；如果不部署 NeuralHash 或 PhotoDNA 等工具，Apple 就无法履行建立 CSAM 保护措施的责任。

sentences.en: Both NeuralHash and PhotoDNA were built to monitor and report violative images uploaded to company servers.
sentences.zh: NeuralHash 和 PhotoDNA 都是为了监控和报告上传到公司服务器的违规图像而构建的。

sentences.en: Yet just the decision regarding whether to deploy either tool is a choice related to content moderation.
sentences.zh: 然而，是否部署任一工具的决定只是与内容审核相关的选择。

blocks.html_en: Also, Apple didn’t fail to satisfy any duty to report items to NCMEC if it never identified CSAM in the first place.

sentences.en: Also, Apple didn’t fail to satisfy any duty to report items to NCMEC if it never identified CSAM in the first place.
sentences.zh: 此外，如果苹果一开始就没有发现 CSAM，那么它也没有履行向 NCMEC 报告项目的任何义务。

blocks.html_en: The Lemmon v. Snap workaround fails: “all of Plaintiffs’ claims here are inexorably linked to third-party content; Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple created content like a Snapchat filter that caused them harm.” The Roommates.com workaround also fails: “Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple modified or augmented the CSAM on its servers in any way.”

sentences.en: The Lemmon v.
sentences.zh: 莱蒙诉。

sentences.en: Snap workaround fails: “all of Plaintiffs’ claims here are inexorably linked to third-party content; Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple created content like a Snapchat filter that caused them harm.”
sentences.zh: Snap 解决方案失败了：“原告在此提出的所有主张都与第三方内容密切相关；原告并未指控苹果创建了 Snapchat 过滤器等内容，对他们造成了伤害。”

sentences.en: The Roommates.com workaround also fails: “Plaintiffs do not allege that Apple modified or augmented the CSAM on its servers in any way.”
sentences.zh: Roommates.com 的解决方法也失败了：“原告并未指控苹果以任何方式修改或增强了其服务器上的 CSAM。”

blocks.html_en: The case reaches its inevitable denouement of a win for Apple. However, Judge Wise remains troubled about its implications. She expresses her uneasiness in stronger-than-normal terms:

sentences.en: The case reaches its inevitable denouement of a win for Apple.
sentences.zh: 此案不可避免地以苹果获胜的结局告终。

sentences.en: However, Judge Wise remains troubled about its implications.
sentences.zh: 然而，怀斯法官仍然对其影响感到不安。

sentences.en: She expresses her uneasiness in stronger-than-normal terms:
sentences.zh: 她用比平时更强烈的措辞表达了她的不安：

blocks.html_en: As it stands, nothing in the law prevents any company, including Apple, from utilizing available technology or creating new technology to identify and report child pornography stored and distributed on their traditional servers or through their cloud services. Conversely, there is no law that obligates companies to proactively do so. Undoubtedly any such legislation would come at a cost of at least some loss of privacy for millions of people. But if lawmakers expected that companies would take steps to prevent their products from being used for storing and distributing child pornography based on something short of a legal imperative, this case, like many others before it, demonstrates the inadequacy of that approach. If lawmakers want to ensure that Apple and other companies address their role in the dissemination of CSAM, they must require it under the law. In other words, lawmakers can fix this problem that is contributing to the exploitation of children.

sentences.en: As it stands, nothing in the law prevents any company, including Apple, from utilizing available technology or creating new technology to identify and report child pornography stored and distributed on their traditional servers or through their cloud services.
sentences.zh: 就目前情况而言，法律中没有任何内容禁止包括苹果在内的任何公司利用现有技术或创造新技术来识别和报告在其传统服务器或通过其云服务存储和分发的儿童色情内容。

sentences.en: Conversely, there is no law that obligates companies to proactively do so.
sentences.zh: 相反，没有法律强制公司有义务主动这样做。

sentences.en: Undoubtedly any such legislation would come at a cost of at least some loss of privacy for millions of people.
sentences.zh: 毫无疑问，任何此类立法都会以至少数百万人丧失部分隐私为代价。

sentences.en: But if lawmakers expected that companies would take steps to prevent their products from being used for storing and distributing child pornography based on something short of a legal imperative, this case, like many others before it, demonstrates the inadequacy of that approach.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果立法者期望公司根据法律要求采取措施，防止其产品被用于存储和传播儿童色情内容，那么本案与之前的许多其他案件一样，表明了这种方法的不足。

sentences.en: If lawmakers want to ensure that Apple and other companies address their role in the dissemination of CSAM, they must require it under the law.
sentences.zh: 如果立法者希望确保 Apple 和其他公司解决其在传播 CSAM 方面的角色，他们必须根据法律提出要求。

sentences.en: In other words, lawmakers can fix this problem that is contributing to the exploitation of children.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，立法者可以解决这个导致剥削儿童的问题。

blocks.html_en: As it stands, nothing in the law prevents any company, including Apple, from utilizing available technology or creating new technology to identify and report child pornography stored and distributed on their traditional servers or through their cloud services. Conversely, there is no law that obligates companies to proactively do so. Undoubtedly any such legislation would come at a cost of at least some loss of privacy for millions of people. But if lawmakers expected that companies would take steps to prevent their products from being used for storing and distributing child pornography based on something short of a legal imperative, this case, like many others before it, demonstrates the inadequacy of that approach. If lawmakers want to ensure that Apple and other companies address their role in the dissemination of CSAM, they must require it under the law. In other words, lawmakers can fix this problem that is contributing to the exploitation of children.

sentences.zh: 但是，如果立法者期望公司根据法律要求采取措施，防止其产品被用于存储和传播儿童色情内容，那么本案与之前的许多其他案件一样，表明了这种方法的不足。

sentences.zh: 如果立法者希望确保 Apple 和其他公司解决其在传播 CSAM 方面的角色，他们必须根据法律提出要求。

sentences.en: In other words, lawmakers can fix this problem that is contributing to the exploitation of children.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，立法者可以解决这个导致剥削儿童的问题。

blocks.html_en: She goes through this framing pretty quickly, but we should slow it down. The “loss of privacy for millions of people” she briefly references deserves a little more care. The opinion downplays the encryption angle; it mentions encryption only twice, as if it’s an afterthought. However, encryption is the critical attribute underlying Apple’s moves. Forcing Apple to scan for CSAM in iCloud means breaking end-to-end encryption for everyone and all purposes, including: malefactors who would intercept private files for criminal purposes; and government actors who have repeatedly shown that they will break into, distribute, and weaponize privately stored file caches (I was just thinking about the North Korea Sony hack. Remember it?).

sentences.en: She goes through this framing pretty quickly, but we should slow it down.
sentences.zh: 她很快就完成了这个框架，但我们应该放慢速度。

sentences.en: The “loss of privacy for millions of people” she briefly references deserves a little more care.
sentences.zh: 她简短提到的“数百万人失去隐私”值得更多关注。

sentences.en: The opinion downplays the encryption angle; it mentions encryption only twice, as if it’s an afterthought.
sentences.zh: 该意见淡化了加密角度；它只提到了两次加密，就好像这是事后才想到的。

sentences.en: However, encryption is the critical attribute underlying Apple’s moves.
sentences.zh: 然而，加密是苹果此举的关键属性。

sentences.en: Forcing Apple to scan for CSAM in iCloud means breaking end-to-end encryption for everyone and all purposes, including: malefactors who would intercept private files for criminal purposes; and government actors who have repeatedly shown that they will break into, distribute, and weaponize privately stored file caches (I was just thinking about the North Korea Sony hack.
sentences.zh: 强迫 Apple 扫描 iCloud 中的 CSAM 意味着为所有人和所有目的破坏端到端加密，包括：出于犯罪目的拦截私人文件的犯罪分子；政府行为者一再表明他们将闯入、分发和武器化私人存储的文件缓存（我只是在考虑朝鲜索尼黑客事件。

sentences.en: Remember it?).
sentences.zh: 还记得吗？）。

blocks.html_en: So yes, the “privacy loss” Judge Wise mentions indeed would be a major cost to everyone. Worse, it would ensure new victims have their sensitive, private, or abusive images nonconsensually intercepted and disseminated. (Do you recall the Fappening and Snappening?) End-to-end encryption isn’t just some nice-to-have feature; it is one of the core planks of a technology architecture that keeps people safer.

sentences.en: So yes, the “privacy loss” Judge Wise mentions indeed would be a major cost to everyone.
sentences.zh: 所以，是的，怀斯法官提到的“隐私损失”确实会给每个人带来重大成本。

sentences.en: Worse, it would ensure new victims have their sensitive, private, or abusive images nonconsensually intercepted and disseminated.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，这将确保新受害者的敏感、私人或辱骂性图像在未经同意的情况下被拦截和传播。

sentences.en: (Do you recall the Fappening and Snappening?)
sentences.zh: （你还记得《Fappening》和《Snapening》吗？）

sentences.en: End-to-end encryption isn’t just some nice-to-have feature; it is one of the core planks of a technology architecture that keeps people safer.
sentences.zh: 端到端加密不仅仅是一个可有可无的功能；它是让人们更安全的技术架构的核心支柱之一。

blocks.html_en: Judge Wise continues:

sentences.en: Judge Wise continues:
sentences.zh: 怀斯法官继续说道：

blocks.html_en:
This Order does not turn on whether Apple’s decisions contributed to Plaintiffs’ injuries. All Plaintiffs’ claims are founded on Apple serving as a publisher of third-party content. It is that role as “publisher” that is dispositive on the issue of immunity. This does not mean that the existence of images and videos of the putative class members being sexually abused—content that they allege is regularly stored and disseminated on iCloud—has not caused Plaintiffs real and lasting harm…

the outcome also adds credence to claims that the Ninth Circuit “has expanded § 230(c)’s scope to provide functional immunity to internet companies, even when they are aware (or should be aware) of unlawful content on their websites.” The practical outcome is that the current state of the law prioritizes privacy—a laudable and critically important value given that in our modern world nearly all our most personal and intimate data (including financial and health records) are stored and transmitted online. But the law should not ignore how those who create, view, and distribute child pornography leverage privacy protections to avoid detection by law enforcement. In the current legal framework, there is no protection for members of the putative class—individuals who as children were photographed and filmed while being abused in the vilest ways imaginable, and who now are repeatedly victimized each time the intimate and tortured images of their trauma are distributed to others. Those children are the collateral damage of our ineffective legal landscape. They deserve better.

sentences.en: This Order does not turn on whether Apple’s decisions contributed to Plaintiffs’ injuries.
sentences.zh: 该命令并未涉及苹果公司的决定是否导致原告受伤。

sentences.en: All Plaintiffs’ claims are founded on Apple serving as a publisher of third-party content.
sentences.zh: 所有原告的主张均基于苹果公司作为第三方内容的发布者。

sentences.en: It is that role as “publisher” that is dispositive on the issue of immunity.
sentences.zh: 正是“发布者”的角色在豁免问题上具有决定性作用。

sentences.en: This does not mean that the existence of images and videos of the putative class members being sexually abused—content that they allege is regularly stored and disseminated on iCloud—has not caused Plaintiffs real and lasting harm…
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着假定集体成员遭受性虐待的图像和视频的存在（他们声称这些内容定期在 iCloud 上存储和传播）并未对原告造成真正和持久的伤害……

sentences.en: the outcome also adds credence to claims that the Ninth Circuit “has expanded § 230(c)’s scope to provide functional immunity to internet companies, even when they are aware (or should be aware) of unlawful content on their websites.”
sentences.zh: 这一结果还进一步证实了第九巡回法院“扩大了第 230(c) 条的范围，为互联网公司提供功能豁免，即使它们知道（或应该知道）其网站上存在非法内容。”

sentences.en: The practical outcome is that the current state of the law prioritizes privacy—a laudable and critically important value given that in our modern world nearly all our most personal and intimate data (including financial and health records) are stored and transmitted online.
sentences.zh: 实际结果是，目前的法律优先考虑隐私——这是一个值得称赞且至关重要的价值，因为在我们的现代世界中，几乎所有我们最个人和最私密的数据（包括财务和健康记录）都是在线存储和传输的。

sentences.en: But the law should not ignore how those who create, view, and distribute child pornography leverage privacy protections to avoid detection by law enforcement.
sentences.zh: 但法律不应忽视那些创作、观看和传播儿童色情内容的人如何利用隐私保护来避免执法部门的发现。

sentences.en: In the current legal framework, there is no protection for members of the putative class—individuals who as children were photographed and filmed while being abused in the vilest ways imaginable, and who now are repeatedly victimized each time the intimate and tortured images of their trauma are distributed to others.
sentences.zh: 在当前的法律框架中，对假定阶层的成员没有任何保护——这些人在孩童时期就被以可以想象到的最卑鄙的方式虐待时被拍照和拍摄，而现在，每当他们的创伤的亲密和折磨的图像被分发给其他人时，他们就会一再成为受害者。

sentences.en: Those children are the collateral damage of our ineffective legal landscape.
sentences.zh: 这些孩子是我们无效的法律环境的附带损害。

sentences.en: They deserve better.
sentences.zh: 他们值得更好。

It is that role as “publisher” that is dispositive on the issue of immunity. This does not mean that the existence of images and videos of the putative class members being sexually abused—content that they allege is regularly stored and disseminated on iCloud—has not caused Plaintiffs real and lasting harm…

But the law should not ignore how those who create, view, and distribute child pornography leverage privacy protections to avoid detection by law enforcement. In the current legal framework, there is no protection for members of the putative class—individuals who as children were photographed and filmed while being abused in the vilest ways imaginable, and who now are repeatedly victimized each time the intimate and tortured images of their trauma are distributed to others. Those children are the collateral damage of our ineffective legal landscape. They deserve better.

sentences.zh: 这一结果还进一步证实了第九巡回法院“扩大了第 230(c) 条的范围，为互联网公司提供功能豁免，即使它们知道（或应该知道）其网站上存在非法内容。”

sentences.zh: 实际结果是，目前的法律优先考虑隐私——这是一个值得称赞且至关重要的价值，因为在我们的现代世界中，几乎所有我们最个人和最私密的数据（包括财务和健康记录）都是在线存储和传输的。

sentences.zh: 但法律不应忽视那些创作、观看和传播儿童色情内容的人如何利用隐私保护来避免执法部门的发现。

sentences.en: In the current legal framework, there is no protection for members of the putative class—individuals who as children were photographed and filmed while being abused in the vilest ways imaginable, and who now are repeatedly victimized each time the intimate and tortured images of their trauma are distributed to others.
sentences.zh: 在当前的法律框架中，对假定阶层的成员没有任何保护——这些人在孩童时期就被以可以想象到的最卑鄙的方式虐待时被拍照和拍摄，而现在，每当他们的创伤的亲密和折磨的图像被分发给其他人时，他们就会一再成为受害者。

sentences.en: Those children are the collateral damage of our ineffective legal landscape.
sentences.zh: 这些孩子是我们无效的法律环境的附带损害。

sentences.en: They deserve better.
sentences.zh: 他们值得更好。

blocks.html_en: Judge Wise isn’t well-situated to compare the relative strengths and limitations of the full range of potential anti-CSAM options, but a leading tool has always been and remains the government’s efforts to find and prosecute the creators, disseminators, and downloaders of CSAM. (Do you recall the federal government’s choices that leave children more vulnerable?) Making sure the government is doing what it can should be the #1 priority. In contrast, breaking end-to-end encryption shouldn’t be anywhere in the regulatory toolkit.

sentences.en: Judge Wise isn’t well-situated to compare the relative strengths and limitations of the full range of potential anti-CSAM options, but a leading tool has always been and remains the government’s efforts to find and prosecute the creators, disseminators, and downloaders of CSAM.
sentences.zh: Judge Wise 无法很好地比较所有潜在反 CSAM 选项的相对优势和局限性，但政府一直并仍然致力于寻找和起诉 CSAM 的创建者、传播者和下载者，这是一个领先的工具。

sentences.en: (Do you recall the federal government’s choices that leave children more vulnerable?)
sentences.zh: （您还记得联邦政府让儿童变得更加脆弱的选择吗？）

sentences.en: Making sure the government is doing what it can should be the #1 priority.
sentences.zh: 确保政府尽其所能应该是第一要务。

sentences.en: In contrast, breaking end-to-end encryption shouldn’t be anywhere in the regulatory toolkit.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，破解端到端加密不应该出现在监管工具包的任何地方。

blocks.html_en: Case Citation: Amy v. Apple Inc., 2026 WL 2031817 (N.D. Cal. July 13, 2026). The complaint.

sentences.en: Case Citation: Amy v.
sentences.zh: 案例引用：艾米诉。

sentences.en: Apple Inc., 2026 WL 2031817 (N.D.
sentences.zh: Apple Inc.，2026 WL 2031817（ND 号）

sentences.en: Cal.
sentences.zh: 卡尔。

sentences.en: The complaint.
sentences.zh: 投诉。

content_html_zh:
此案涉及苹果对私有 iCloud 存储中托管的用户上传文件的处理。 Apple 没有采用 PhotoDNA 扫描托管文件中的 CSAM，而是创建了自己的专有替代方案 NeuralHash，但显然效果不太好。 因此，Apple 开始在其云存储中扫描 CSAM。 相反，Apple 对 iCloud 文件实施了端到端加密。

苹果公司的举动让公众感到困惑，对于苹果公司来说，这似乎是一个令人尴尬的非受迫性错误。 它还确保了来自政府和原告（包括 CSAM 受害者）的压力，他们更喜欢苹果更具干预性的做法，而苹果已经自愿表明愿意这样做。

这起诉讼是对苹果和 230 条款的全面攻击。 “原告声称，苹果未能实施任何已知的 CSAM 检测是一种设计缺陷，因为苹果可以安全地实施现成的功能来防止已知 CSAM 的传播，但一直未能这样做。” 之前的博客文章。 法院驳回了第三次修改后的申诉，该申诉将案件提交第九巡回法院审理，在那里（像往常一样）任何事情都可能发生。

* * *

法院重申第 230 条适用于原告的索赔：

首先，原告的主张将苹果视为 CSAM 内容的发布者或发言人，这些内容激起了原告的伤害。 从根本上说，原告认为，苹果公司本来可以（并且在他们看来应该）使用现成的技术来防止传播描绘此类推定类别中的原告的儿童色情内容，但苹果公司已选择允许用户传播和共享第三方 CSAM 内容。 原告寻求援引“被告作为出版商的身份”的义务，因此“适用豁免权”。 其次，豁免权也适用，因为“避免责任的方法需要[苹果]充当发行商。” 因此，Apple 有权根据第 230 条享有完全豁免。

引用 Doe 1 v. 元，法院说：

原告的伤害是第三方使用 iCloud 共享 CSAM 行为的直接结果，苹果既没有明确宽恕也没有阻止这种使用（即使假设——正如 TAC 中所指控的——苹果知道为此目的使用 iCloud）……尽管原告声称苹果知道其工具可能被用来传播儿童色情内容（正如本案中心的苹果内部短信所证实的那样），但根据当前的法律状况，苹果仍然有权获得豁免§ 230——无论常识如何……。 原告无法避免这样一个事实：检测 CSAM 的工具必须审查 CSAM 才能做出这样的决定。 尽管苹果本可以采取措施来做到这一点（正如其竞争对手通过使用 PhotoDNA 所做的那样），但 Grindr 确认，§ 230 禁止苹果因设计决策而提出的索赔，只要这些索赔与苹果促进他人沟通和内容的作用有关

（提醒一下，被告的科学家与第 230 条无关）。

原告试图适应 Doe v. 案中创建的新的第 230 条例外。 Twitter，但法院拒绝了此举：

本案不涉及、甚至不讨论苹果的内容报告系统；它涉及苹果“未能针对 CSAM 传播实施行业标准保障措施”。 尽管报告系统和 CSAM 保障措施都可以被描述为“缺陷”，但后者要求法院将 Apple 视为发行商。 Twitter“可以履行其声称的职责，解决报告基础设施的缺陷，而无需监控、删除或以任何方式参与第三方内容”；如果不部署 NeuralHash 或 PhotoDNA 等工具，Apple 就无法履行建立 CSAM 保护措施的责任。 NeuralHash 和 PhotoDNA 都是为了监控和报告上传到公司服务器的违规图像而构建的。 然而，是否部署任一工具的决定只是与内容审核相关的选择。

此外，如果苹果一开始就没有发现 CSAM，那么它也没有履行向 NCMEC 报告项目的任何义务。

莱蒙诉。 Snap 解决方案失败了：“原告在此提出的所有主张都与第三方内容密切相关；原告并未指控苹果创建了 Snapchat 过滤器等内容，对他们造成了伤害。” Roommates.com 的解决方法也失败了：“原告并未指控苹果以任何方式修改或增强了其服务器上的 CSAM。”

* * *

此案不可避免地以苹果获胜的结局告终。 然而，怀斯法官仍然对其影响感到不安。 她用比平时更强烈的措辞表达了她的不安：

就目前情况而言，法律中没有任何内容禁止包括苹果在内的任何公司利用现有技术或创造新技术来识别和报告在其传统服务器或通过其云服务存储和分发的儿童色情内容。 相反，没有法律强制公司有义务主动这样做。 毫无疑问，任何此类立法都会以至少数百万人丧失部分隐私为代价。 但是，如果立法者期望公司根据法律要求采取措施，防止其产品被用于存储和传播儿童色情内容，那么本案与之前的许多其他案件一样，表明了这种方法的不足。 如果立法者希望确保 Apple 和其他公司解决其在传播 CSAM 方面的角色，他们必须根据法律提出要求。 换句话说，立法者可以解决这个导致剥削儿童的问题。

她很快就完成了这个框架，但我们应该放慢速度。 她简短提到的“数百万人失去隐私”值得更多关注。 该意见淡化了加密角度；它只提到了两次加密，就好像这是事后才想到的。 然而，加密是苹果此举的关键属性。 强迫 Apple 扫描 iCloud 中的 CSAM 意味着为所有人和所有目的破坏端到端加密，包括：出于犯罪目的拦截私人文件的犯罪分子；政府行为者一再表明他们将闯入、分发和武器化私人存储的文件缓存（我只是在考虑朝鲜索尼黑客事件。 还记得吗？）。

所以，是的，怀斯法官提到的“隐私损失”确实会给每个人带来重大成本。 更糟糕的是，这将确保新受害者的敏感、私人或辱骂性图像在未经同意的情况下被拦截和传播。 （你还记得《Fappening》和《Snapening》吗？） 端到端加密不仅仅是一个可有可无的功能；它是让人们更安全的技术架构的核心支柱之一。

怀斯法官继续说道：

该命令并未涉及苹果公司的决定是否导致原告受伤。 所有原告的主张均基于苹果公司作为第三方内容的发布者。 正是“发布者”的角色在豁免问题上具有决定性作用。 这并不意味着假定集体成员遭受性虐待的图像和视频的存在（他们声称这些内容定期在 iCloud 上存储和传播）并未对原告造成真正和持久的伤害…… 这一结果还进一步证实了第九巡回法院“扩大了第 230(c) 条的范围，为互联网公司提供功能豁免，即使它们知道（或应该知道）其网站上存在非法内容。” 实际结果是，目前的法律优先考虑隐私——这是一个值得称赞且至关重要的价值，因为在我们的现代世界中，几乎所有我们最个人和最私密的数据（包括财务和健康记录）都是在线存储和传输的。 但法律不应忽视那些创作、观看和传播儿童色情内容的人如何利用隐私保护来避免执法部门的发现。 在当前的法律框架中，对假定阶层的成员没有任何保护——这些人在孩童时期就被以可以想象到的最卑鄙的方式虐待时被拍照和拍摄，而现在，每当他们的创伤的亲密和折磨的图像被分发给其他人时，他们就会一再成为受害者。 这些孩子是我们无效的法律环境的附带损害。 他们值得更好。

Judge Wise 无法很好地比较所有潜在反 CSAM 选项的相对优势和局限性，但政府一直并仍然致力于寻找和起诉 CSAM 的创建者、传播者和下载者，这是一个领先的工具。 （您还记得联邦政府让儿童变得更加脆弱的选择吗？） 确保政府尽其所能应该是第一要务。 相比之下，破解端到端加密不应该出现在监管工具包的任何地方。

案例引用：艾米诉。 Apple Inc.，2026 WL 2031817（ND 号） 卡尔。 2026 年 7 月 13 日）。 投诉。
